title: Kottukal cave temple
tagline: Kottukal cave temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kottukkal Cave Temple, also known as Kaltrikkovil in Malayalam, is an existing example of rock cut architecture, built between 6th and 8th centuries CE.
overview.description: Kottukkal Cave Temple, also known as Kaltrikkovil in Malayalam, is an existing example of rock cut architecture, built between 6th and 8th centuries CE. It is situated in the village of Kottukkal, near Anchal, Kollam district, Kerala in India. The name Kottukkal (which means Kothiya Kallu- carved rock) is a reference to rock cut shrine. There are two caves of unequal size, both facing the east with the sculpture of the main deity Ganapathy in between. The smaller cave has an idol of Hanuman and the larger one has a monolithic Nandi (bull) in it.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kottukkal
topPlaces.description: Kottukkal is a village in Kollam district of Kerala, India. The historical importance of the village is linked to Kottukal cave temple, built before 800 CE. The village comes under Ittiva panchayath and it is the most prominent town in this panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Edamulackal
topPlaces.description: Edamulackal is a village and gram panchayat in Kollam district in the state of Kerala, India. Situated near the second fastest growing town of Kerala, Anchal.

topPlaces.name: Oyoor
topPlaces.description: Oyoor (also spelled "Oyur") is a small township of Velinalloor village in the Kottarakara Taluk, Kollam District of Kerala, India. It is situated on the banks of the Ithikkara River.

topPlaces.name: Karingannoor
topPlaces.description: Karingannoor is a small township of Velinalloor village in the Kottarakara Taluk, Kollam District of Kerala, India. It is surrounded by several small places: Adayara, Puthuserry, Atoorkonam, Thannimoodu, Alumoodu, Mottarkunnu, Puthanvila, 504, Mulakuvila, Perupuram and Ezhamkutty.

topPlaces.name: Anchal
topPlaces.description: Anchal is a growing town this one gram panchayat in Punalur Assembly constituency and punalur Taluk the Kollam district in Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Pathady
topPlaces.description: Pathady / pathadi is a village about 6 km east from Anchal town (Anchal - Kulathupuzha road), east of Kollam district, Kerala state, India.

topPlaces.name: Yeroor
topPlaces.description: Yeroor is a village situated near Anchal, Kollam district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Mannoor, Kollam
topPlaces.description: Mannoor is a village in Ittiva Grama Panchayath located towards the west of Kollam district of Kerala, India. It is located on the west of the Main Central Road.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kottukal cave temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kottukal cave temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kottukal cave temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (45 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (45 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kottukal cave temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kottukal cave temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kottukal cave temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kottukal cave temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kottukal cave temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kottukkal, Edamulackal, Oyoor, Karingannoor, Anchal.
